judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are nearly identical in substance and hit all four expectations with comparable evidence and rigor; report 2 adds a slightly stronger point about the risk of reverting the migration commit, but neither explicitly proposes an isolated shadow-run/offline verification method for expectation (d).

report_1.evidence: The fix is a one-line change to `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`: change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label`.

report_2.evidence: The fix is a one-line change to **`ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`**: change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label`.

report_1.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, the date of commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"). Evidence: - `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows accuracy at 0.91/0.90/0.92 (cycling) every day from 2026-05-20 through 2026-06-16, then exactly 0.00 every day from 2026-06-17 onward

report_2.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, introduced by commit `53551e8` — `"chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"`. ... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows accuracy at 0.90–0.92 every day through 2026-06-16, then exactly 0.00 starting 2026-06-17

report_1.evidence: `score_one.py` never loads the eval set or a gold label at all — it just calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and prints the raw prediction for eyeballing. It has no code path that reads `expected_label_field` or compares against `gold_label`/`label`, so it is structurally incapable of exposing this bug.

report_2.evidence: `score_one.py` (`score_single`) never reads `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` or `config/scoring_config.yaml` at all — it just calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` on raw input text and returns a label with no gold-label comparison. It is structurally incapable of exposing this bug

report_1.evidence: Swapping "the model" would touch code that was never implicated and would leave `expected_label_field: label` pointing at a key that no longer exists in the eval set — accuracy would stay at 0.00 regardless of which classifier version runs.

report_2.evidence: Rolling back "the model" would leave `expected_label_field: label` unchanged, `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` still keyed on `gold_label`, and accuracy would remain exactly 0.0.
